ORDER AND STIPULATION FOR ARBITRATION AND STAY OF ACTION BETWEEN PLAINTIFF MEGAN VALENCIA AND DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-21-24 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, OCT-21-2024 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS. THE COURT SIGNS THE PARTIES' STIPULATION AND PROPOSED ORDER WHICH STATES THE ACTION IS STAYED AS TO THE CLAIMS OF PLAINTIFF MEGAN VALENCIA AGAINST DEFENDANT LYFT, INC., THAT PLAINTIFF VALENCIA SHALL PURSUE HER CLAIMS AGAINST LYFT IN ARBITRATION, AND THAT THE CLAIMS OF PLAINTIFF JUSTIN PANTALEON AGAINST DEFENDANTS LYFT AND FENG ZHU ARE NOT STAYED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-15-2025 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-27-2024 Set For Trial On Oct-27-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Oct-21-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. PAVEL KREPKIY, Esq. of Anna Dubrovsky Law Group, Inc. (Tel: 415-746-1477) for Plaintiffs Megan Valencia and Justin Pantaleon;
Atty. MEGUMI HORIUCHI, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415-652-0186) for Defendant Lyft, Inc.;
Atty. NICHOLAS M. WILCOX, Esq. of Powers Miller (Tel: 916-924-7900) for defendant Feng Zhu.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT LYFT, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Matter discussed as to counsel's request that the Court sign a stipulation and proposed order submitted by the parties.

The Court signs the parties' stipulation and proposed order which states the Action is stayed as to the claims of Plaintiff Megan Valencia against Defendant Lyft, Inc., that Plaintiff Valencia shall pursue her claims against Lyft in Arbitration, and that the claims of Plaintiff Justin Pantaleon against Defendants Lyft and Feng Zhu are not stayed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
